tweets.title: Bangladesh High Commissioner Riaz Hamidullah pays homage to Photographer Raghu Rai. Among the many iconic images he clicked, he covered the liberation war of Bangladesh brought to fore the genocide by West Pakistan army in 1971.
tweets.content:
Bangladesh High Commissioner Riaz Hamidullah pays homage to Photographer Raghu Rai. Among the many iconic images he clicked, he covered the liberation war of Bangladesh brought to fore the genocide by West Pakistan army in 1971.

Riaz Hamidullah (@hamidullah_riaz)

My dear RAGHU bhai (@RaghuRai1), I won't ever hear your heartfelt voice again over phone, greeting me in immaculate Urdu and filling void with your signature warmth!

Last (2025) April, within two weeks of joining in Delhi, a call comes to me from him, "Reyaz bhai, I dare address you as 'bhai', hope you would grant me that allowance...". He sends his book, BIRTH OF A NATION, thru his daughter (based on all rare negatives of 1971 War that he misplaced in the pile of his archive).

He was one of my childhood heroes. Many #youth who may not know: in August 1971, a 29-year-old photo-journalist in @TheStatesmanLtd, Raghu Rai, entered #Bangladesh to cover 🇧🇩Liberation War. He shared with me accounts of intense suffering, agony and humiliation of #people that he witnessed in battlefield - villages - Refugee Camps. Raghu bhai narrated to me a chilling account of an ambush when he kept clicking even braving splinters!  

What he saw on the ground reminded him of his childhood when his family had to cross over to India (1947) braving so much of uncertainties. He was barely 5, then.
In 1972, 🇮🇳 Govt. awarded a young Raghu Rai #PadmaShri for his epochal photography on 🇧🇩War.

Since April '25, Raghu bhai embraced me just as a younger brother. A giant of man in the world of #photography #art #aesthetics, each time I would call him, he would greet me on phone almost like hugging me. Words just won't suffice.

Raghu bhai, in eight decades, you touched so many souls, across lands. You didn't belong just to #India but to the world.
Adieu, Raghu bhai. RIP.

@IndiaToday

Who was the brave unsung hero Tahir Fazal Chaudhary and why he was honoured by Indian army ?

Tahir Fazal Chaudhary, also known as "Bahadur-e-Hill Kaka," an unsung hero from Op Sarp Vinash, passed away very recently, on April 22, 2026, at the age of 62 due to a heart attack in Uttarakhand. 16RR paid tribute by performing full military honors.

In 2002, Tahir was working in Saudi Arabia when he received news that his brother, Haji Mohammad Arif, had been brutally killed by militants. He immediately quit his job and returned to Poonch, taking a solemn oath to avenge his brother and liberate his homeland. 

After His Return To India
He mobilized local youth to stand against foreign terrorists and formed ‘Pir Panjal Scouts,' initially funded by himself, who jointly worked with 9 Para SF & 16 RR.

His Role in OP Sarp Vinash
As a local, he became the eye, ear, and heart of the Indian Army. Where the Army helicopter couldn’t land, he used to provide the precise location of the hideout to foot soldiers of the Indian Army, which led to the killing of 80+ terrorists and dismantled the whole terrorist network in the Pir Panjal mountains.

Aftermath Of His Role
Tahir killed terrorist commander Kassid, who killed his brother, and kept his AK-47 as a trophy of war for life.

Eventually, the leopard gave up and moved towards the fields.

Through his courage, he managed to save his daughter from the claws of death.

RT by @NetramDefence: 🚨🇮🇳 Full blockade of the Strait of Hormuz might choke India?  

Not so fast. India has a powerful ace up its sleeve: the Chennai-Vladivostok Eastern Maritime Corridor.  

This ambitious sea route is now one of the top strategic priorities for both Russia and India. It’s designed to slash dependence on traditional chokepoints and unlock direct, reliable trade between the two nations.  

The corridor will boost joint projects in Russia’s Far East while dramatically expanding trade in crude oil, metals, machinery and equipment.  

Signed back in September 2019 at the Eastern Economic Forum, the route stretches over 10,000 kilometres. It officially began operations in early 2024 and is already changing the game.  

While the world watches tensions in the Middle East, India and Russia have built a vital alternative that keeps energy and goods flowing no matter what happens in Hormuz.

EU Plans New Energy Routes to Bypass Hormuz Risk, The bloc is also considering long-term projects like the India–Middle East–Europe corridor to diversify supply chains.

The European Union is exploring alternative energy infrastructure in the Middle East to reduce reliance on the Strait of Hormuz, amid disruption caused by the ongoing conflict

Ursula von der Leyen said the EU is ready to partner with Gulf nations to build new export routes and repair damaged energy facilities, aiming to avoid critical choke points.
